title: Peppery Juicy Slovakian Kielbasa Pierogi
description: Date-night Slovakian dish featuring juicy kielbasa, vanilla custard, and a hint of pepper. Perfect for low-carb diets.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi according to package instructions or until they float.
In a large skillet, cook the kielbasa over medium-high heat, browning on both sides and crisping the skin.
In a separate saucepan, heat the carmelized onions and sliced bacon over low heat until crispy.
Assemble the pierogi by placing a spoonful of the filling mixture onto a piece of dough, then sealing and boiling.
In a separate pan, melt the butter over medium heat and sauté the chopped fresh parsley until wilted.
Serve the pierogi hot, topped with vanilla custard, sour cream, carmelized onions, and crispy bacon.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and freshly ground black pepper, and serve immediately.
Prepare the ghee or butter for cooking, keeping it warm and ready to use.
Cook the pierogi dough according to package instructions and let it rest before assembling the pierogi.
Prepare the carmelized onions and sliced bacon in advance, letting them cool before storing them in an airtight container.
Prepare the vanilla custard according to package instructions, keeping it refrigerated until serving.
Prepare the pierogi filling mixture with kielbasa, carmelized onions, and sliced bacon before assembling the pierogi.
